metadata.title: Bear Brown Logo — Every Remotion Move.
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Spanish. claude-liam channel. Liam in for Bear.
metadata.note: Technique-showcase reel: the Bear Brown full logo mark animated every way Remotion knows. 22 beats B00–B22. Audio-first, free pipeline only (Kokoro am_onyx). No ElevenLabs, no Higgsfield, no publishing. Logo: logos/bear-brown-logo-black.svg, viewBox 0 0 7500 5000, one complex path + one rect background. All paths, no live text.

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. One logo. Twenty techniques. I'm going to run the Bear Brown mark through every motion move Remotion knows — so you can see which ones actually work.
Spring Entrance. Default spring, pop-in from nothing. Watch how it settles — is there enough weight, or does it feel cheap?
Overshoot Spring. High stiffness, low damping. The mark slams in, squashes on landing, bounces back. Physical, or just noise?
Draw-On Stroke. evolvePath traces the outline in terracotta, then the fill floods in. Pen feel, or loading bar?
Mask Reveal. Clip-path wipes left to right, then an iris opens from the center. Two reveal modes, one beat.
Scale Zoom. Linear first — uniform, indifferent. Then bezier — the landing actually curves. The easing is the difference.
Rotation. Spring pivot entrance, then a slow continuous hold. Meditative when it's right. Aimless when it isn't.
Skew and Shear. Spring-driven lean to eighteen degrees, hold, release. Italic tension — or wobble. You decide.
Opacity Through Blur. Slow spring, eighteen pixels of blur clearing to zero as the mark arrives. It comes in out of focus.
Color Interpolation. This is the treatment beat. Ink cycles through terracotta and back. Signal, or decoration?
Kinetic Grid. The mark tiles across the frame, each copy on a staggered delay, then a ripple rolls through. Grid becomes rhythm.
Glitch Slices. Six horizontal slices, each offset independently on a deterministic seed. Aggressive for eight frames, then clean.
Trail Echo. Five ghost copies trail behind at four-frame lag each. The opacity tapers. Motion lingers.
Noise Wobble. Sine-driven X, Y, and rotation jitter. Amplitude decays from eight pixels to zero. Alive, or anxious?
Elastic Physics. Spring drop from two-fifty pixels above. Squash burst fires on contact. It hits the floor.
Card Flip. Perspective rotateY springs to zero, then a slow second pass. Depth, or squash? The card decides.
Shadow Play. An independent shadow layer stretches ahead of the mark, up to sixty pixels, then snaps back. Detached, then grounded.
Composer Summon. Prompt types in the Claude UI. Send button arms. Terracotta spark fires. The mark springs in from below.
Stroke Pulse. Dashoffset cycles like a heartbeat. The outline breathes. On every beat, the rhythm is visible.
Scale Breathe. Continuous sine oscillation — one percent expansion and contraction, forever. The logo inhales.
Exit Family. Shrink-spin, blur-out, iris close — three exits in equal thirds. Which one earns the frame?
Your turn. Take your own logo SVG. Run it through spring entrance, draw-on stroke, and exit family. Six seconds each. Keep or kill.
Liam, in for Bear.
